title: CloudWatch Logs Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Log Groups, Metric Filters, and AWS native logging.

questions.question: How are logs organized fundamentally within AWS CloudWatch?

options.text: By Region mapping directly to raw text files.
By Folders containing zipped CSV files.
By IAM Roles and Policies.
By Log Groups (the application level) which contain multiple Log Streams (the instance/container level).

questions.question: Which of the following AWS services automatically sends its `stdout` log output to CloudWatch Logs with zero configuration required?

options.text: RDS PostgreSQL
EC2 Instances
S3 Buckets
AWS Lambda Serverless Functions

questions.question: If you want to trigger an automated PagerDuty alert every time the word 'Timeout' appears more than 5 times in a log file, what CloudWatch feature do you use?

options.text: You install an ELK stack instead.
You write a Lambda function to parse the logs.
Create a Metric Filter to count the pattern, then create a CloudWatch Alarm tied to that metric.
You use CloudTrail.
A machine learning predictive algorithm.
A monthly PDF billing report.
An automated security scanner.
An interactive query language and tool within the AWS Console used to search and analyze terabytes of log data quickly.

questions.question: Why do many DevOps teams export older CloudWatch Logs to Amazon S3 rather than keeping them in CloudWatch indefinitely?

options.text: CloudWatch forcibly deletes unencrypted logs every 24 hours.
CloudWatch Logs is significantly more expensive for massive, long-term multi-year storage compared to S3/Glacier.
CloudWatch is not compliant with GDPR.
S3 allows faster searching using Kibana.
